'''Shaun Berrigan''' (born 4 November 1978) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk|ce|hb}} and {{rlp|fe}} in the 1990s, 2000s, and 2010s.

He played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]], with whom he won the [[2000 NRL Grand Final|2000]] and [[2006 NRL Grand Final]], and the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. He also played for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]]. Berrigan played for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] in the [[State of Origin series]] and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at international level.

==Background==
Berrigan was born in [[Brisbane]], Queensland, Australia on 4 November 1978. He is of Italian and German descent.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleague.com/rugby-league-news/german-rl-shines-in-the.html |title=Archived copy |access-date=2009-10-17 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110715213443/http://www.rugbyleague.com/rugby-league-news/german-rl-shines-in-the.html |archive-date=15 July 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/sport/nrl/story/0,26746,21203349-5003409,00.html| work=The Sunday Mail (Qld)| title=Berrigan could go| first=Peter| last=Badel| date=2007-02-10}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/berrigan-on-azzurris-radar-for-world-cup-20121027-28d1t.html | title=Berrigan on Azzurri's radar for World Cup| date=28 October 2012}}</ref> His brother [[Barry Berrigan|Barry]] was also a fellow professional, with whom he played in the Toowoomba Rugby League competition for Dalby Diehards in the 2014 season.

==Playing career==
===Brisbane Broncos===
Berrigan made his premiership début for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in Round 11 of the [[1999 NRL season]] against the [[Balmain Tigers]], making eleven appearances during the remainder of the aforementioned NRL season. In the following season in 2000, Berrigan played from the interchange bench in the Broncos' [[2000 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Having won the [[2000 NRL Premiership]], the Broncos traveled to England to play against 2000's [[Super League V]] Champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.]] for the [[2001 World Club Challenge]], with Berrigan playing at five-eighth and scoring a try in Brisbane's loss. Berrigan's regular position was {{rlp|ce}} but he could play a number of back-line positions as well as {{rlp|hk}}. Berrigan's versatility is evident in analysing the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland team]] for which Berrigan was the five-eighth for the entire [[2002 State of Origin series]]. He played the entire [[2003 State of Origin series]] at halfback for Queensland, who lost. He played in the centres for the [[2004 State of Origin series]]. Berrigan was selected in the Australian team to go and compete in the end of season [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] he played at centre in the Kangaroos' 44–4 victory.

Berrigan was then the top try-scorer for the [[2005 Brisbane Broncos season]] scoring 19 tries. Berrigan played his 150th first grade game for the club in [[Brisbane Broncos 2006|2006]]. He started the year in the centre position but injuries to the Broncos' full-time hookers, [[Michael Ennis]] and Shaun's brother [[Barry Berrigan|Barry]], made coach [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] move Berrigan to hooker. This turned out to be a success, with former great [[Peter Sterling (rugby league commentator)|Peter Sterling]] saying that it bought a new dimension to the Broncos' game not seen for a while.{{Citation needed|date=January 2011}} He played so well at hooker in the Broncos' [[2006 NRL Grand Final]] victory over Melbourne that he was awarded the [[Clive Churchill Medal]] for best on field.<ref>{{cite news
| last = Stevenson
| first = Andrew
| title = Medal for the quiet achiever
| work =  The Sydney Morning Herald
| date = 2006-10-02
| url = https://www.smh.com.au/news/league/medal-for-the-quiet-achiever/2006/10/01/1159641217277.html
| access-date = 2009-12-16}}</ref> Berrigan was superb from [[Rugby league positions#dummy half|dummy half]], but also shut down [[Melbourne Storm]] super-star [[Greg Inglis]] for the entire match. He was later selected in the 2006 [[Rugby League Tri-Nations|Tri-Nations]] side for Australia and played in the final which Australia won. In the 2006 and 2007 Origin series Berrigan has been the utility back for Queensland on the bench, playing the majority of these games as Hooker when [[Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)|Cameron Smith]] was off the field.  As 2006 NRL Premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]] travelled to England to face 2006 [[Super League]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Berrigan played at {{rlp|hk}} in the Broncos' 14–18 loss.

Berrigan was selected to play for the [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian national team]] from the interchange bench in the 2007 [[ANZAC Test]] match victory against New Zealand.<ref>{{cite web|title=Anzac Test Match 2007|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/anzac-test-match-2007/australia-vs-new-zealand.html|work=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|access-date=1 September 2013}}</ref> During the [[2007 NRL season]], at the Broncos' 20-year anniversary celebration, the club announced a list of the 20 best players to play for them to date which included Berrigan.<ref>{{cite news|last=Dekroo|first=Karl|title=Still the king|work=The Courier-Mail|place=Australia|publisher=Queensland Newspapers|date=2007-05-09|url=http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,21701434-10389,00.html|access-date=2009-12-08|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070512101607/http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,21701434-10389,00.html|archive-date=12 May 2007}}</ref> Berrigan's [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] contract ended at the end of the 2007 season.<ref name="Contracts not a distraction for Broncos">{{cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/Sport/Contracts-not-a-distraction-for-Broncos/2007/05/29/1180205227101.html|title=Contracts not a distraction for Broncos|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2007-05-29|access-date=2007-05-31}}</ref>

Berrigan was a regular representative player, appearing twelve times in the State of Origin for the Queensland Maroons and in five [[Test match (rugby league)|test match]]es for Australia. 
[[File:Shaun Berrigan, Hull FC.jpg|thumb|right|Berrigan playing for Hull F.C. in 2008]]

===Hull FC===
Berrigan signed a four-year deal with English [[Super League]] team and 2006 Grand Final runners up, Hull FC.<ref name="HULL FC SIGN AUSSIE SUPERSTAR">{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/index.php?/news/more/hull-fc-sign-aussie-superstar/|title=HULL FC SIGN AUSSIE SUPERSTAR|publisher=hullfc.com|date=2007-06-14|access-date=2007-06-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927222954/http://www.hullfc.com/index.php?%2Fnews%2Fmore%2Fhull-fc-sign-aussie-superstar%2F|archive-date=27 September 2007|url-status=dead}}</ref> His contract commenced with the start of the 2008 season when he replaced former Broncos teammate [[Richard Swain]], who retired from the game at the end of the 2007 season, thus freeing up a free quota space.<ref name="HULL BOOSTED BY BERRIGAN CAPTURE">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/06/14/RUGBYL_Hull.html|title=HULL BOOSTED BY BERRIGAN CAPTURE|publisher=Sportinglife|date=2007-06-14|access-date=2007-06-14}}</ref>

In regards to the signing, Berrigan said "I have had a fantastic career at the Broncos, winning premierships, playing for Queensland and of course for Australia. I am really excited to be joining Hull and playing in the [[Super League]] and [[Challenge Cup]]. I am excited by their plans, I believe the club has a fantastic future and I am looking forward to being a part of it for the next 4 years".<ref name="Kangaroo Berrigan to join Hull FC">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/6752283.stm|title=Kangaroo Berrigan to join Hull FC|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-14|access-date=2007-06-14}}</ref><ref name="HULL FC SIGN SHAUN BERRIGAN">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=28373|title=HULL FC SIGN SHAUN BERRIGAN|publisher=RLeague.com|date=2007-06-14|access-date=2007-06-14|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930230222/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=28373|archive-date=30 September 2007}}</ref>

Berrigan scored his first try for new club Hull in Round 2 of 2008's [[Super League XIII]]. On 30 August Shaun Berrigan appeared for Hull at Wembley in the [[2008 Challenge Cup Final]]. During his Hull career Berrigan continued to play at {{rlp|hk}}.

===New Zealand Warriors===
At the start of 2011, Berrigan secured a release from Hull so he could move to be closer to his family following the death of his father in 2010. After being linked with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and [[Gold Coast Titans]], Berrigan signed a one-year deal with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] for the [[2011 New Zealand Warriors season|2011 season]]. During the 2011 NRL season, Berrigan played from the bench and at centre, although he did not play in their grand final loss against Manly.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=38198 Former Kangaroo Berrigan Joins Warriors] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110112214442/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=38198 |date=12 January 2011}} ''rleague.com'', 9 January 2011</ref>

===Canberra Raiders===
On 19 October 2011, the [[Canberra Raiders]] announced they had signed Berrigan on a one-year contract for the 2012 season with the option for an extension.{{citation needed|date=April 2023}}

He announced his retirement at the end of the [[2013 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jaspasro |first=By |date=6 August 2013 |title=Family comes first for retiring Berrigan |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/central-queensland/family-comess-first-for-retiring-star-berrigan/news-story/26be67efee95d72923953d8d86922699 |access-date=8 August 2024 |website=couriermail.com.au}}</ref>

== Post playing ==
After retiring from the sport in 2013, Berrigan had moved to work in the mines.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-09-28 |title=Prop turned lifesaver; club greats working mines: Where Broncos' '06 Grand Final team is now |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-grand-final-2023-brisbane-broncos-2006-where-are-they-now-what-do-the-players-do-for-work-justin-hodges-darius-boyd-shaun-berrigan-shane-perry/news-story/17be8f173816c3e146609575a276b381 |access-date=2024-08-10 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref> In 2023, Berrigan played in the FOG side that versed the Queensland Police Service Rugby League Association.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-02-21 |title=Legends out in force to honour police heroes |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2023/02/22/legends-out-in-force-to-honour-police-heroes/ |access-date=2024-08-10 |website=Queensland Rugby League |language=en}}</ref>
